channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of service
item.description: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.

item.title: Twitter Just Can’t Stop Leaking Movies
item.description: If you're still hanging around on Twitter, you've probably seen 'Avatar 3' and 'The Super Mario Galaxy' leaks on there. Second verse...

item.title: Los Thuthanaka’s Wak’a is a mellower follow-up to last year’s surprise Pitchfork favorite
item.description: Los Thuthanaka basically came out of nowhere last year to capture Pitchfork's album of the year with their self-titled debut. Because it wasn't available on streaming, it largely flew under the radar. I honestly kind of forgot about it until Pitchfork gave it the number one spot in its year-end list. In retrospect, I'm not […]

item.title: 'The most powerful weapon is not always a missile': How Iranian "Charming Kitten" hackers used old Cold War methods to steal tech secrets and plant malware on Apple and Windows users
item.description: Iran’s Charming Kitten group relies on deception, insider access, and low-tech methods to steal trade secrets and compromise systems.

item.title: Claude, OpenClaw and the new reality: AI agents are here — and so is the chaos
item.description: The age of agentic AI is upon us — whether we like it or not. What started with an innocent question-answer banter with ChatGPT back in 2022 has become an existential debate on job security and the rise of the machines. More recently, fears of reaching artificial general intelligence (AGI) have become more real with the advent of powerful autonomous agents like Claude Cowork and OpenClaw. Having played with these tools for some time, here is a comparison.First, we have OpenClaw (formerly known as Moltbot and Clawdbot). Surpassing 150,000 GitHub stars in days, OpenClaw is already being deploye...

item.title: As people look for ways to make new friends, here are the apps promising to help
item.description: We've compiled a list of friendship apps, from friend discovery platforms like BFF to event-focused ones like Timeleft.

item.title: Light up your life with the Philips Hue Omniglow, the best Hue lightstrip yet
item.description: Light up your life with the Philips Hue Omniglow, the best Hue lightstrip yet, which appears simply seamless.

item.title: J’ai testé un smartphone 4G en 2026 et il est loin d’être ridicule : Xiaomi Redmi Note 15 4G
item.description: Un smartphone 4G en 2026, oui ça existe encore. Il ne fait pas le café, mais si vous avez un budget très serré et ne consommez que des réseaux sociaux et du streaming vidéo, il a des arguments.

item.title: The UK government reportedly wants Anthropic to expand its presence in London
item.description: While the US and Anthropic are in the midst of a major dispute, the UK is trying to sway the San Francisco-based AI company to expand its presence on English soil. According to a report from The Financial Times, staffers at the UK's Department for Science, Innovation and Technology have worked on proposals that include expanding Anthropic's office in London, along with a potential dual stock listing. The UK's strategy follows a public fallout between Anthropic and the US Department of Defense earlier this year. After the AI company said it wouldn't budge on certain AI guardrails, the Departmen...

item.title: La récente Blink Outdoor 2K+, qui surveille l’extérieur de votre cocon, est bradée à moitié prix
item.description: Compacte, discrète et riche en fonctionnalités intelligentes, la Blink Outdoor 2K+ est une caméra de surveillance connectée très complète. Actuellement, en 2026, Amazon la propose, avec son module de synchronisation, à 49,99 euros au lieu de 99,99 euros.

item.title: 6,5 Go de textures dans moins d’un Go : la magie noire de Nvidia arrive et c’est bien plus prometteur que le DLSS 5
item.description: Faire tenir 6,5 Go de textures haute définition dans seulement 970 Mo de VRAM.

item.title: Quels sont les meilleurs TV 4K 75 pouces en 2026 ?
item.description: Marre de votre petit téléviseur ? Craquez donc pour une grande TV de 75 ou de 77 pouces. Que ce soit pour un film, un match de foot ou pour jouer, l'immersion ne sera que meilleure !
